steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence: "Though it was designated as mission ______ mission was actually the ninety-fourth flight under NASA's Space Shuttle Program." Left of blank: dependent clause — "Though it was designated as mission STS-96." Right of blank: independent clause — "the mission was actually the ninety-fourth flight." Rule: a dependent clause followed by an independent clause requires a comma between them.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — STS-96, and the: "Though it was designated as mission STS-96, and the mission was actually..." "And" is a coordinating conjunction for two independent clauses, but "Though it was designated..." is a dependent clause — the "and" here is a mismatch that distorts the contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — STS-96. The: "Though it was designated as mission STS-96. The mission was actually..." A period ends the sentence after a dependent clause — that dependent clause cannot stand alone as a sentence; this creates a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — STS-96 the: "Though it was designated as mission STS-96 the mission was actually..." No punctuation between dependent and independent clause — the boundary is missing, creating a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — STS-96, the: "Though it was designated as mission STS-96, the mission was actually the ninety-fourth flight." Comma after the dependent clause correctly separates it from the following independent clause. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
